title: Fix off-by-one in Cursor::advance so it stops at EOF
instructions: Cursor::advance in crates/theseus-lexer/src/cursor.rs reads one byte past the end of the input on the final token, producing a spurious trailing token. Fix advance() so it returns None (and the lexer emits EOF) exactly at the input boundary. Edit cursor.rs in place; do not add new files or move code out of this file. Do NOT run cargo or nix yourself — the mechanical gate builds and runs the acceptance tests independently; get it right by reading the code carefully. If you believe the fix genuinely requires touching a file outside the allowlist, STOP and report why instead of editing out of scope.

acceptance_criteria.check: The lexer emits exactly one EOF token and no trailing spurious token for a non-empty input ending without a newline.
